headlineSummary: Hacker News sentiment skews negative toward Google Gemini, with comments calling out weaker coding, high token use, and outdated outputs, while Anthropic gets more positive reactions for coding usefulness and OpenAI sees a small positive note [E2][E3][E4][E5][E6][E7].

ranking.summary: Negative sentiment centers on Gemini being weaker than Claude Code, burning through tokens, and giving outdated or incorrect guidance on recent iOS details [E2][E3][E4].
OpenAI has limited evidence and a mild positive note from praise for Codex’s broad developer-system knowledge and easy setup [E5].

Anthropic draws positive sentiment from praise for Opus 4.5 generating useful wrappers quickly and from a comment highlighting Claude being used successfully to code Claude, despite one pricing complaint [E1][E6][E7].

evidence.excerpt: Just when you thought it was safe to use Opus 4.5 at 1/3 the cost, they go and add a 6x 'bank-breaking mode' - So now accidental bankruptcy is just one toggle away.

annotations.rationale: Uses Anthropic model alias Opus 4.5 and strongly criticizes the new fast mode as excessively expensive.

evidence.excerpt: Though it's worse at coding, and Gemini CLI doesn't have the agentic strength of Claude Code, yet.

annotations.rationale: Directly says Gemini is worse at coding and that Gemini CLI is weaker agentically than Claude Code.
Praises Claude Code by stating Gemini CLI lacks its agentic strength and implying Claude performs better at coding.

evidence.excerpt: I need to give this a try, but nowadays I am reluctant to fire up Gemini CLI due to its insane appetite for tokens. It doesnt matter if your LLM in/out tokens are a bit cheaper than competitors when you use 3x of them on every prompt. Maybe Google should focus on addressing that first?

annotations.rationale: Explicitly criticizes Gemini CLI for excessive token usage and poor effective cost efficiency.

evidence.excerpt: I can relate to this. Gemini 3 doesn’t know a thing about iOS 26 or Liquid Glass. It constantly assumes this is some custom view that I want it to develop and ends up building something out the previous gen apis like ultrathinmaterial.

annotations.rationale: States Gemini 3 lacks relevant knowledge and repeatedly generates outdated, incorrect solutions.

evidence.excerpt: Codex seems to have innate knowledge of how to curl jira and confluence and gitlab and prometheus and SQL databases and more. All you need to configure is a .netrc file and put the hostname in AGENTS.md.

annotations.rationale: Praises Codex as broadly capable and easy to configure for many developer systems.

evidence.excerpt: I gave Opus 4.5 the registers and it produced wrapper functions that I could pass to LVGL in a few minutes, requiring three prompts. This is just one of countless examples where I've basically stopped using libraries for anything that isn't LVGL, TinyUSB, compression or cryptography. The purpose built wrappers Opus can make are much smaller, often a bit faster, and perhaps most significantly not encumbered with the mental model of another developer's assumptions about how people should use their library.

annotations.rationale: Strongly endorses Opus 4.5 as highly effective for generating useful, efficient code quickly.

evidence.excerpt: At the same time, Anthropic is successfully coding Claude using Claude.

annotations.rationale: Explicitly claims Anthropic is successfully using Claude to code Claude, a strong endorsement of model capability.
